TWO TREES REPORTED DOWN EAST OF HEADLAND.
WATERSPOUT BRIEFLY CAME ASHORE AND SPUN A SHERIFF PATROL CAR AROUND. NO INJURIES WERE REPORTED.

A WEAK EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST EAST OF ALABAMA HIGHWAY 87. THE NEXT AREA OF DAMAGE WAS REPORTED EAST NORTHEAST FROM THE INITIAL TOUCH DOWN ALONG COUNTY ROAD 611 WHERE A BARN WAS DESTROYED ALONG WITH SEVERAL TREES SNAPPED. THE FINAL AREA OF DAMAGE WAS REPORTED NORTH OF ALABAMA HIGHWAY 134 WHERE A ROOF WAS TAKEN OFF THE PORCH OF A HOUSE ALONG WITH A PORTION OF THE ROOF OVER THE KITCHEN. THE OVERALL PATH WIDTH OF DAMAGE WAS AROUND 75 YARDS. THE MAXIMUM WINDS WERE 80 MPH OR EF-0 STRENGTH.
TORNADO REPORTED ON THE GROUND AT THE INTERSECTION OF AL87 AND HWY 134.
TORNADO TOUCHDOWN AT A RESIDENCE IN NEW BROCKTON ALAMABA. DAMAGE TO THE ROOF AND THE PORCH WAS HEAVILY DAMAGED.

AN NWS SURVEY CREW IN CONJUNCTION WITH COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT FOUND THE DAMAGE IN THIS LOCATION TO BE CONSISTENT WITH AN EF-0 TORNADO WHICH TOUCHED DOWN JUST SOUTHWEST OF FLORIDA HIGHWAY 267 AND TRACKED NORTHWEST ONTO HOLT LANE. DAMAGE SOUTHWEST OF HOLT LANE WAS LIMITED TO TREES SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. A FEW STRUCTURES WERE DAMAGED ON HOLT LANE. ONE OUTBUILDING LOST ITS ROOF. THE TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST CROSSING I-10 NEAR EXIT 181. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 1 MILE WITH A WIDTH OF 75 YARDS.
5 STRUCTURES DAMAGED ON HOLT LANE JUST SOUTH OF INTERSTATE 10. 1 STRUCTURE SUFFERED MAJOR DAMAGE. NO INJURIES OR FATALITIES REPORTED. MORE INFORMATION TO FOLLOW.
TORNADO TOUCHDOWN 3 MILES SOUTH OF INTERSTATE 10 ALONG HWY 267. MANY TREES DOWN...FURTHER DAMAGE WILL BE INVESTIGATED.
60 FOOT GREENHOUSE DESTROYED BY DOWNBURST. TIME OF EVENT ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
DAMAGE REPORTED TO THE BP GAS STATION NEAR LLOYD JUST SOUTH OF INTERSTATE 10. A FEW TREES REPORTED DOWN IN THE AREA AS WELL.

THE NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE SURVEYED THE AREA AROUND LLOYD FLORIDA AT INTERSTATE 10 NEAR EXIT 215. THE DAMAGE IN THIS AREA WAS LIMITED TO A FEW TREES AND AN AWNING TO A GAS STATION JUST OFF FLORIDA HIGHWAY 59. THE DAMAGE IN THIS AREA WAS DEEMED CONSISTENT WITH STRAIGHT LINE WINDS AND ESTIMATED AROUND 65 MPH.

NUMEROUS TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN COUNTY WIDE. DAMAGE LASTED FROM 445 PM TO ABOUT 5 PM EDT.

ALTHOUGH OFFICIAL RUNWAY WIND SENSOR WAS INOP...THE BACKUP SENSOR MEASURED 76 MPH. OBSERVER ON DUTY SAID IT WAS ONE OF THE WORST STORMS HE HAS SEEN. TOWER OBSERVERS ESTIMATED WINDS NEAR 70 MPH. PILOTS IN A-10 AIRCRAFT REPORTED SEVERE SHAKING BUT NO DAMAGE.
